description: Drama requires element configurations rooted in interpersonal conflict, emotional stakes, and irreversible choices — the engine is human relationships under pressure, not external threats.

character_constraints.description: A character with a deep emotional stake who must make irreversible interpersonal decisions — an agent, not a passenger.
An opposing figure whose conflict with the protagonist is rooted in relationship, not villainy — a spouse, parent, friend, or colleague.
A bystander or confidant whose presence raises the emotional stakes of confrontation.

relationship_constraints.description: Conflict rooted in a close relationship — the antagonism is personal and relational, not external.
A relationship where one character observes and judges another's choices.

place_constraints.description: A setting that forces proximity and prevents easy escape — home, courtroom, hospital, institution.
The arena where the central confrontation unfolds — emotionally charged and specific.

object_constraints.description: A document, ring, letter, or will that carries the emotional stakes in physical form.
An object that surfaces buried history — photograph, recording, diary entry.

element_rules.description: Every major beat must involve an interpersonal decision — the protagonist must act as agent in relationships, not merely react to external events.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least 3 timeline transitions are initiated by the protagonist making a relational choice.

element_rules.description: An irreversible choice scene and an aftermath scene are both mandatory — the drama must cross a point of no return.
element_rules.testable_condition: The timeline contains at least one 'chooses' transition that cannot be undone and a subsequent scene showing consequences.
